narrative:
Pete Crow-Armstrong headlines today’s slate as our clear anchor, capitalizing on a stellar matchup against Miles Mikolas with a projected 111.57 score, bolstered by favorable BvP history and tailwinds. He is supported by high-upside plays in Trea Turner and Colt Keith, both of whom benefit significantly from platoon advantages and premium lineup spots. Keith, in particular, looks poised to exploit his matchup against Randy Vásquez, with a massive +30% SP skill adjustment driving his 77.22 projection.

While the core is strong, we face minor headwinds from local weather, as the "Wind In" factor is dampening the output of five of our starters, including Kyle Schwarber and Yordan Alvarez. Schwarber remains a start despite the L/L platoon penalty due to his elite xwOBA, but Alvarez’s projection has dipped significantly against Detmers, requiring close monitoring. Carson Benge is our "historical edge" pivot; despite a poor park factor, his +15% BvP boost against Mitch Keller makes him a high-efficiency contrarian play to round out the middle of the order.
